item.title: Architect Bjarke Ingels Says Modern Buildings Are So Boring
item.description: A favorite of eco-friendly urban planners and the technocrat class, the Danish designer has been getting by on government and donor-subsidized projects as shareholders keep purse strings tight.

item.title: Australia’s New South Wales State to Prohibit New Coal Mines
item.description: New South Wales, Australia’s second-biggest coal-producing state, will ban new mine applications for the fossil fuel.

item.title: Rio Shuts Bauxite Mines as Cyclone Hits Northern Australia
item.description: Rio Tinto Group shut two bauxite mines as tropical cyclone Narelle struck Australia’s Northern Queensland coast on Friday morning, lashing the region with destructive winds and heavy rains.

item.title: Fervo Plans IPO as Geothermal Escapes Trump’s Clean Energy Attacks
item.description: The startup’s rise has mirrored rising energy demand, driven by the data center boom.

item.title: States Take Trump EPA to Court Over Endangerment Finding Repeal
item.description: More than 20 states are legally challenging the Trump administration’s decision to roll back a landmark scientific determination that greenhouse gases threaten human health, which underpins various federal climate rules.

item.title: KKR-Backed Company Bets on Electric Trucks Despite Trump Attacks
item.description: Following its entry in California, Zenobe is shopping for electric truck assets in states including Illinois, New York and Massachusetts.

item.title: Electric Speed Boat Maker Expands to Defense With New Funding
item.description: Arc received backing from a16z, Lowercarbon Capital and other venture firms in its latest fundraising round.

item.title: Chile Cools on Climate, Penguin Defense in Risk to Green Credits
item.description: President José Antonio Kast’s pledge to ease environmental regulations to boost investment threatens to have unintended consequences for Chile — pushing up borrowing costs in a nation that has pioneered green debt sales in the region.

item.title: The Future of Climate Science Without the US
item.description: Jim Skea, chair of the UN’s climate science body, discusses the future of its crucial research after the US pulled its support.

item.title: Geothermal Escapes Trump's Renewable Crackdown, Boosts Fervo's IPO Bid
item.description: Fervo Energy is also benefiting from AI’s ravenous electricity needs as it aims to go public later this year.

item.title: Weeks of War Are Reshaping the Global Gas Market for Years to Come
item.description: The industry is staring down prolonged disruptions in the Persian Gulf. Emerging economies are already paying the price.

item.title: Oil Giants Bid $163 Million in Trump’s Record Alaska Lease Sale
item.description: ConocoPhillips and Exxon Mobil Corp. were among oil companies that snapped up drilling leases in northern Alaska’s mammoth petroleum reserve in a record auction that drew $163 million in high bids.

item.title: Meeting Surging Demand for AI Memory Chips Has a Climate Cost
item.description: The rush to boost production of memory chips to meet fast accelerating demand from artificial intelligence will add to the semiconductor sector’s climate footprint and risks lifting costs of managing emissions.

item.title: US Home Insurance Costs Are Rising Faster Than Inflation
item.description: The average annual premium is projected to increase 4% to about $3,057 this year.

item.title: European Consumers Seek Out Solar, EVs as Energy Prices Surge
item.description: Companies in the UK and Germany say they have received more inquiries for green technologies since the Iran war started.

item.title: BMW’s Best-Selling Model Is Getting an Electric Reboot
item.description: For decades, it’s been BMW AG’s most popular model, a symbol of professional success that allowed owners to show off to their neighbors. Now, the 3-Series sedan is being remade for the electric age.

item.title: AI Boom Drives US to Build Enough Battery Systems for Domestic Demand
item.description: The US now has the production capacity to supply 100% of its energy-storage systems domestically, a key milestone in the push to boost American battery manufacturing and decrease the nation’s reliance on Chinese imports.

item.title: UK Greens Want Tax Rise on Oil, Wealth to Help Ease Energy Bills
item.description: Britain’s resurgent Green Party will call on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to hike taxes on businesses and the wealthy in order to subsidize voters’ household bills, in light of soaring energy prices from the US war on Iran.

item.title: Oil, Gas Majors Cut Green Spending for First Time Since 2017
item.description: The world’s largest oil and gas companies ratcheted back investment in the energy transition in 2025, marking the first annual decline in eight years, according to BloombergNEF.

item.title: US Home Insurance Prices Set to Keep Rising With Severe Weather
item.description: California is projected to see the biggest jump, while Florida is the most expensive market overall.

item.title: Singapore Wants Empty Bottles Back to Reduce Plastic Waste
item.description: Don’t throw out that plastic bottle. The Singapore government wants empty beverage containers in return for cash, in a bid to boost the recycling rate.

item.title: Major Tropical Cyclone Threatens Mine Region in North Australia
item.description: A major storm is strengthening off Australia’s northern coast, threatening to bring destructive winds and heavy rains as it heads for landfall in the North Queensland mining region later this week.

item.title: EVs Avoided the Use of 2.3 Million Barrels of Oil Per Day in 2025
item.description: With gas prices climbing due to the Iran War, interest in electrified transportation is on the rise.

item.title: Chile’s Kast Halts Green Decrees in Rush to Undo Boric Legacy
item.description: Chile’s new government moved to withdraw dozens of environmental decrees issued under the previous administration, a sweeping move that threatens to damage the Latin American nation’s green credentials as it looks to boost investment.

item.title: Los Angeles, US West Brace for Second Bout of Record March Heat
item.description: Los Angeles and the US Southwest are set for a second record heat wave in two weeks as temperatures soar, potentially straining electric grids, melting snowpack and raising health risks.

item.title: 在阿拉斯加建造新煤电厂的十亿美元交易
item.description: 特朗普政府大力推动煤炭产业，这可能导致美国自2013年以来首座新电厂的建成。【原文：The Trump administration’s push for coal could lead to the first new US plant to be built since 2013.】

item.title: 欧洲的绿色能源革命缓解了伊朗能源价格冲击
item.description: 欧洲电力市场正面临自2022年能源危机以来首次严峻的地缘政治压力测试——而迄今为止，市场仍保持稳定。【原文：Europe’s power market is facing its first serious geopolitical stress test since the 2022 energy crisis — and, so far, it’s holding up.】

item.title: 巴基斯坦太阳能发电量意外增长，缓解了中东能源冲击
item.description: 巴基斯坦在过去几年中迅速推广太阳能发电，这有助于缓解中东战争导致化石燃料价格飙升带来的影响。【原文：Pakistan’s rapid adoption of solar power in the past few years is helping cushion the impact of a surge in fossil fuel prices due to the Middle East war.】

item.title: 巴西在修订后的气候计划中加大力度保护森林
item.description: 巴西周一公布了更新后的国家气候计划，这是该国自2008年以来首次修订其旨在遏制二氧化碳排放并适应全球变暖的主要政策。【原文：Brazil on Monday unveiled an updated national climate plan, the first time since 2008 the country has revised its main policy to curb carbon-dioxide emissions and adapt to global warming.】

item.title: 美国计划在阿拉斯加建设自2013年以来首个新建燃煤电厂
item.description: Terra Energy Center正向阿拉斯加一项拟建的煤炭项目投入10亿美元，这是十多年来美国首个针对新建燃煤电厂的投资。【原文：Terra Energy Center is pouring $1 billion into a deal for a planned coal project in Alaska, marking the first investment in new US coal power in more than a decade.】
